news.title: Geelong’s refinery fire may be out – but questions over what it means for our fuel crisis are still raging
news.snippet: Viva executives and firefighters will try to determine the cause of the blaze while the PM will hope the disruption to Australia's supplies is minimal.

news.title: Fire breaks out at crucial Australian refinery, raising fuel supply fears
news.snippet: Gas leak ignites Viva fuel plant, which produces about 10 percent of Australia's fuel, in Geelong, Victoria. Listen (4 mins).

news.title: Australian Fuel Supply to Get Even Tighter After Refinery Fire

news.title: Firefighters battle huge blaze at Australian oil refinery
news.snippet: Officials say there were no suspicious circumstances behind the fire at the Viva Energy Geelong oil refinery, one of only two in Australia.

news.title: 'Unprecedented' fire at Australian oil refinery to affect nation's petrol supplies

news.title: Blaze tears through Australian refinery; disruptions feared

news.title: What does the Geelong refinery fire reveal about Australia’s fuel supplies?
news.snippet: This fire should prompt us - again - to consider the future role of liquid fuels in Australia.

news.title: Video. Australia: Refinery fire raises fuel supply concerns amid Iran war
news.snippet: Video. Fire at Viva Energy's Geelong refinery, which supplies 10% of Australia's fuel, sparks supply fears as Iran war keeps global prices elevated.

news.title: Sky News Australia. . Prime Minister Anthony Albanese questioned on fuel supply after the Geelong oil refinery fire, from petrol production to 60 per cent.
